ddls:
CREATE TABLE "experiments" (
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each experiment */,
  "experiment_name" TEXT /* Name of the experiment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the experiment */,
  "experimental_design" TEXT /* Experimental design or condition (e.g., between-subjects or within-subjects) */,
  "experimental_protocol" TEXT /* Experimental protocol or procedure (e.g., a detailed description of the experiment) */,
  "expected_outcomes" TEXT /* Expected outcomes or hypotheses of the experiment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("experiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "participants" (
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each participant */,
  "participant_name" TEXT /* Name of the participant */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the participant */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the participant */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level of the participant */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the participant */,
  "handedness" TEXT /* Handedness of the participant (left-handed, right-handed, or ambidextrous) */,
  "native_language" TEXT /* Native language of the participant */,
  "medical_history" TEXT /* Medical history of the participant */,
  "psychological_history" TEXT /* Psychological history of the participant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("participant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instructions" (
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instruction */,
  "instruction_text" TEXT /* Text of the instruction */,
  "instruction_type" TEXT /* Type of the instruction (e.g., text-based, image-based, or audio-based) */,
  "instruction_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the instruction (in milliseconds) */,
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the experiment the instruction is for */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stimuli" (
  "stimulus_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stimulus */,
  "stimulus_type" TEXT /* Type of the stimulus (e.g., visual, auditory) */,
  "stimulus_size" INTEGER /* Size of the stimulus (in pixels) */,
  "stimulus_color" TEXT /* Color of the stimulus */,
  "stimulus_shape" TEXT /* Shape of the stimulus */,
  "stimulus_orientation" TEXT /* Orientation of the stimulus */,
  "stimulus_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the stimulus (in milliseconds) */,
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the experiment the stimulus is for */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stimulus_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "response_type" TEXT /* Type of the response (e.g., button press, vocal response) */,
  "response_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the response (in milliseconds) */,
  "response_accuracy" INTEGER /* Accuracy of the response */,
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the experiment the response is for */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trials" (
  "trial_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trial */,
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the experiment the trial is part of */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the participant who took the trial */,
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the instruction given in the trial */,
  "stimulus_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the stimulus presented in the trial */,
  "isi" INTEGER /* Inter-stimulus interval in milliseconds */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the response given by the participant */,
  "accuracy" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the response was accurate */,
  "reaction_time" INTEGER /* Reaction time of the participant in milliseconds */,
  "instruction_onset" INTEGER /* Onset time for the instruction in milliseconds */,
  "stimulus_onset" INTEGER /* Onset time for the stimulus in milliseconds */,
  "response_onset" INTEGER /* Onset time for the response in milliseconds */,
  "trial_type" TEXT /* Type of the trial (e.g., practice trial or actual trial) */,
  "trial_number" INTEGER /* Trial number (for experiments with multiple trials) */,
  "trial_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the trial (in milliseconds) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trial_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trials_experiment_id FOREIGN KEY ("experiment_id") REFERENCES experiments ("experiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trials_instruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_id") REFERENCES instructions ("instruction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trials_stimulus_id FOREIGN KEY ("stimulus_id") REFERENCES stimuli ("stimulus_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trials_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES responses ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trials_participant_id FOREIGN KEY ("participant_id") REFERENCES participants ("participant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "experiment_designs" (
  "experiment_design_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each experimental design */,
  "design_type" TEXT /* Type of the experimental design (e.g., between-subjects or within-subjects) */,
  "num_conditions" INTEGER /* Number of conditions in the design */,
  "condition_names" TEXT /* Names of the conditions (comma-separated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("experiment_design_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stimulus_presentation_order" (
  "stimulus_presentation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stimulus presentation */,
  "stimulus_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the stimulus presented */,
  "presentation_order" INTEGER /* Order in which the stimulus is presented */,
  "isi" INTEGER /* Inter-stimulus interval in milliseconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stimulus_presentation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_stimulus_presentation_order_stimulus_id FOREIGN KEY ("stimulus_id") REFERENCES stimuli ("stimulus_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "experiment_participants" (
  "experiment_participant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each experiment-participant pairing */,
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the experiment */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the participant */,
  "date_completed" TEXT /* Date when the participant completed the experiment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("experiment_participant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_experiment_participants_participant_id FOREIGN KEY ("participant_id") REFERENCES participants ("participant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_experiment_participants_experiment_id FOREIGN KEY ("experiment_id") REFERENCES experiments ("experiment_id")
)
